201:     repo_override: Option<&str>,
202:     force_repoint: bool,
203: ) -> Result<String> {
204:     // Normalize repo shorthand (e.g. "fork" → "owner/repo" from remote URL).
205:     let resolved_override: Option<String> =
206:         repo_override.map(|s| github::resolve_repo_shorthand(s));
207:     // The stored/inherited repo for this branch (before any CLI override).
208:     let stored_repo: Option<String> = state.effective_pr_repo(branch);
209:     // Resolve effective repo: CLI flag > stored per-branch > global config > None
210:     let effective_repo: Option<String> = resolved_override
211:         .clone()
212:         .or_else(|| stored_repo.clone());
213: 
214:     // Resolve the push remote for cross-fork head prefix.
215:     let push_remote = state.effective_push_remote(branch);
216: 
217:     // When force_repoint is set and --repo points to a *different* repo than the
218:     // stored one, look up the existing PR in the OLD repo first so we can close it.
219:     // Without this, the lookup targets the new repo and finds nothing.
220:     let old_repo_for_repoint: Option<String> = if force_repoint {
221:         let old = stored_repo.clone();
222:         if old.is_some() && old != effective_repo { old } else { None }
223:     } else {
224:         None
225:     };
226: 
227:     let existing_pr = if let Some(ref old_repo) = old_repo_for_repoint {
228:         // Look up in the old repo where the PR actually lives.
229:         github::get_pr_status_in_repo(branch, Some(old_repo.as_str()))?
230:     } else {
231:         github::get_pr_status_in_repo(branch, effective_repo.as_deref())?
232:     };
233: 
234:     // Detect cross-repo repoint: if the parent's PR lives in a different repo than
235:     // the current branch's PR, we need to close the old PR and create a new one.
236:     // This happens when sync reparents children after a bottom-of-chain merge and
237:     // the parent chain now targets a different repo, or when --repoint --repo is used.
238:     let (existing_pr, effective_repo) = if let Some(ref pr) = existing_pr {
239:         // The repo where the existing PR actually lives.
240:         let pr_current_repo = old_repo_for_repoint.clone().or_else(|| effective_repo.clone());
241:         // The repo we want the PR to target (from --repo override or parent chain).
242:         let target_repo = if resolved_override.is_some() {
243:             effective_repo.clone()
244:         } else {
245:             state.effective_pr_repo(parent)
246:         };
247:         let needs_repoint = force_repoint || {
248:             // Compare normalized repos: if both are Some but different, repoint needed.
249:             match (&pr_current_repo, &target_repo) {
250:                 (Some(current), Some(target)) => current != target,
251:                 // PR currently in an explicit repo but parent targets the default repo
252:                 // (None means "use gh default") — this is a mismatch if branch has pr_repo.
253:                 (Some(_), None) => state.get_branch(branch).ok().and_then(|m| m.pr_repo.as_ref()).is_some(),
254:                 _ => false,
255:             }
256:         };
257:         let no_repoint = state.effective_no_repoint(branch);
258:         if needs_repoint && !no_repoint {
259:             let old_number = pr.number;
260:             let old_url = &pr.url;
261:             let new_target = target_repo.clone().or_else(|| effective_repo.clone());
262:             ui::info(&format!(
263:                 "Repointing PR #{old_number} — closing in {} and recreating in {}",
264:                 pr_current_repo.as_deref().unwrap_or("default repo"),
265:                 new_target.as_deref().unwrap_or("default repo"),
266:             ));
267:             if let Err(e) = github::close_pr_in_repo(old_number, pr_current_repo.as_deref()) {
268:                 ui::warn(&format!(
269:                     "Could not close old PR #{old_number} ({old_url}): {e}\n  \
270:                      Hint: close it manually and re-run `ez push`"
271:                 ));
272:             }
273:             // Clear stored pr_number and pr_repo so we create fresh.
274:             let meta = state.get_branch_mut(branch)?;
275:             meta.pr_number = None;
276:             meta.pr_repo = None;
277:             // Fall through to the None (create) path with the target repo.
278:             (None, new_target)
279:         } else if needs_repoint && no_repoint {
280:             let old_number = pr.number;
281:             ui::warn(&format!(
282:                 "PR #{old_number} for `{branch}` targets a different repo than parent `{parent}` — \
283:                  skipped repoint (no_repoint is set). Update manually or use `ez push --repoint`."
284:             ));
285:             (existing_pr, effective_repo)
286:         } else {
287:             (existing_pr, effective_repo)
288:         }
289:     } else {
290:         (existing_pr, effective_repo)
291:     };
292:

295:             state.get_branch_mut(branch)?.pr_number = Some(pr.number);
